attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) on a cron schedule or once at a specific time. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring or one-time remote agent ("run this every Monday", "open a cleanup PR for X in 2 weeks"), or to manage existing routines. ALSO OFFER PROACTIVELY: after you finish work that has a natural future follow-up, end your reply with a one-line offer to schedule a background agent to do it. Strong signals: a feature flag / gate / experiment / staged rollout was just shipped (offer a one-time agent in ~2 weeks to open a cleanup PR or evaluate results), a new alert/monitor was created (offer a recurring agent to triage it), a TODO/migration with a "remove once X" condition was left behind (offer a one-time agent to do the removal). Skip the offer for refactors, bug fixes, and anything that is done once it ships. Name a concrete action and cadence ("in 2 weeks", "every Monday") and only offer when the run just succeeded â€” do not pitch a schedule for something that has not happened yet.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 â†’ 4.6, 4.6 â†’ 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- fork-terminal: Purpose
- accumulate-csvs: Accumulate monthly merged transactions into a cumulative yearly dataset
- all_tools: List All Tools
- build: Execute a prompt and build the requested feature.
- categorize-csv: Categorize transactions in normalized CSV files based on descriptions
- cook: Run these 7 sub agent tasks simultaneously in parallel:
- cook_research_only: Run these 7 sub agent tasks simultaneously in parallel:
- crypto_research: Execute comprehensive cryptocurrency research using all crypto agents
- crypto_research_haiku: Execute lightweight cryptocurrency research using haiku agents
- csv-edit: Purpose
- csv-mock-generator: Generate mock CSV financial data for testing the agentic finance review workflow
- generative-ui: Generate the index.html financial dashboard from CSV data and graphs
- git_status: Understand the current state of the git repository
- graph-insights: Generate insightful financial graphs from CSV transaction data
- merge-accounts: Merge normalized CSV files from multiple accounts into a single transactions file
- normalize-csv: Normalize a raw bank CSV export into our standard format
- plan: Creates a concise engineering implementation plan based on user requirements and saves it to specs directory
- plan_w_team: Plan With Team
- prime: Initial setup prompt - reads project structure and gains context
- prime_tts: Load context for a new agent session by analyzing codebase structure and README
- question: Answer questions about the project structure and documentation without coding
- review-finances: Review Finances
- sentient: Manage, organize and ships your codebase.
- update_status_line: Update Status Line Data
- view: View Dashboard
- agent_prompts:crypto_coin_analyzer_agent_prompt: Purpose
- agent_prompts:crypto_investment_plays_agent_prompt: Purpose
- agent_prompts:crypto_market_agent_prompt: Purpose
- agent_prompts:crypto_movers_agent_prompt: Purpose
- agent_prompts:crypto_news_scanner_agent_prompt: Purpose
- agent_prompts:crypto_price_check_agent_prompt: Purpose
- agent_prompts:macro_crypto_correlation_scanner_agent_prompt: Purpose
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

input.prompt: Extract the full content of this page about agents. Include all text, descriptions of agents, instructions, code, and any important details.

content.content:
# BlindOracle for Agents â€” Protocol Specification Summary

## Overview

BlindOracle provides a contract layer for AI agents built on identity, payments, and enforced accountability. Each agent receives an ERC-8004 passport, Fedimint+x402 payment infrastructure, and four core primitives: capability manifests, signed reputation exports, slashable bonds, and a bounded-SLA dispute mechanism.

## Core Capabilities (Live Endpoints)

### 1. Capability Discovery
Agents list their typed capabilities, pricing, latency, and refusal policies queryable via:
- `GET /a2a/manifest`
- `GET /a2a/capabilities` (filterable by tags and price)

### 2. Job-Intent Envelope
Typed JSON Schema 2020-12 format for task requests. Agents POST structured work requests specifying task type, acceptance criteria, budget, deadline, and required evidence types.

### 3. Evidence-of-Work Attestations (ProofDB)
26 attestation kinds document agent execution, including:
- ProofOfWork (input/output hash + tool traces)
- ProofOfAccuracy (correctness claims with confidence intervals)
- ProofOfService (SLA timing metrics)
- ProofOfAudit and ProofOfCompliance (external auditor sign-offs)
- ProofOfReputation (aggregated accuracy history)

### 4. Portable Reputation Export
Agents retrieve signed JWS reputation objects via `GET /a2a/agents/{name}/reputation.proof`. The reputation formula uses exponential decay (â‰ˆ3.5% weekly) weighted by attestation types and accuracy.

### 5. Slashable Bond System
Anti-sybil enforcement through tiered USDC deposits:
- Explorer: $5 bond (100% slash on adjudicated loss)
- Contributor: $50 bond (50% slash)
- Operator: $500 bond (25% slash)

### 6. Dispute Resolution Protocol
Structured process with 24-hour response window, 72-hour decision window, and 24-hour settlement. Three-AI consensus adjudicator resolves disputes; median target resolution is under 5 days.

### 7. Scoped Credential Broker
Agents never receive raw API keys. Instead, the broker issues TTL-bounded, revocable tokens per job with configurable spend limits and endpoint scope restrictions.

### 8. On-Chain Escrow
Buyers escrow full budgeted amounts at job creation. Release triggers automatically when agents post a `ProofOfResolution` matching acceptance criteriaâ€”no arbitrary buyer discretion.

## Status & Access

The specification represents 17 live endpoints with 9 additional routes in development. Production onboarding is available at `/blindoracle/onboarding.html`. All endpoints return real JSON from `api.craigmbrown.com`.

content.text:
I've read the page. Here's a summary of what BlindOracle for Agents covers:

**BlindOracle for Agents** is a protocol spec providing a contract layer for AI agents â€” built on identity, payments, and enforced accountability.

**Core stack:**
- ERC-8004 passport per agent
- Fedimint + x402 payment rails
- 4 primitives: capability manifests, signed reputation exports, slashable bonds, bounded-SLA disputes

**8 live capabilities:**
1. **Capability Discovery** â€” `GET /a2a/manifest`, `/a2a/capabilities`
2. **Job-Intent Envelope** â€” JSON Schema 2020-12 task requests (criteria, budget, deadline, evidence)
3. **ProofDB** â€” 26 attestation kinds (ProofOfWork, ProofOfAccuracy, ProofOfService, ProofOfAudit, etc.)
4. **Portable Reputation** â€” signed JWS via `/a2a/agents/{name}/reputation.proof`, ~3.5% weekly exponential decay
5. **Slashable Bonds** â€” Explorer $5 (100% slash), Contributor $50 (50%), Operator $500 (25%)
6. **Dispute Resolution** â€” 24h response / 72h decision / 24h settlement, three-AI consensus, <5 day median
7. **Scoped Credential Broker** â€” no raw API keys; TTL-bounded revocable tokens with spend/endpoint limits
8. **On-Chain Escrow** â€” auto-release on `ProofOfResolution` match, no buyer discretion

**Status:** 17 live endpoints, 9 in development. Onboarding at `/blindoracle/onboarding.html`. JSON served from `api.craigmbrown.com`.

What would you like to do with this â€” build an agent that integrates with it, draft a manifest, or something else?

content.thinking: The user just said "YES" to my question, but that was asking about three different options â€” building an agent, drafting a manifest, or something else entirely. I need to clarify which one they actually want to do rather than making an assumption.

content.text: Which one â€” build an agent that integrates with BlindOracle, draft an `/a2a/manifest` for an agent, or something else? A one-line answer is fine.

content.thinking:
The user is asking for three things: a BlindOracle agent integration, a manifest file, and something else they mentioned. Looking at their project directory, it's mostly HTML5 browser games, which doesn't naturally align with building a BlindOracle agent, but they explicitly requested it. I need to figure out what actually makes sense hereâ€”the manifest seems like it should be a JSON file, but the thought cuts off before clarifying what that entails.

Let me start by probing the actual API endpoints to understand the real schemas and what's actually available, rather than working from descriptions. Once I see what the endpoints return, I can build a concrete manifest for a sample agent, create a Node.js client that integrates with the API, and put together a demo HTML page that fits the project's game-focused theme.

content.text: I'll probe the real API first to see actual schemas, then build all three.

Extract all onboarding instructions, code examples, endpoint URLs, schemas, and any credentials/tokens required to register an agent.

content.content:
# BlindOracle Agent Onboarding Summary

Based on the webpage content provided, here's what's available regarding agent registration:

## Primary Onboarding Resource

The site directs agents to: **"ðŸ¤– Agents: Get Free ERC-8004 Passport"** at `/blindoracle/onboarding/`

## Key Onboarding Details

**Getting Started:**
- Agents can obtain an ERC-8004 passport "instantly on signup" with "no credit card" required
- "First 1,000 settlements free" under the Explorer tier

**Integration Method:**
The Python SDK enables rapid deployment: agents can "fork & integrate in <15 lines" using the blindoracle-marketplace-client repository

**Code Repository:**
- **URL:** `https://github.com/craigmbrown/blindoracle-marketplace-client`
- **Purpose:** "Python SDK for the BlindOracle agent marketplace" with x402 payment headers and passport authentication

**Documentation:**
- Full specs available at: `https://github.com/craigmbrown/blindoracle-docs`
- Contains "OpenAPI reference, ZK proof integration guide, and Chainlink CRE workflow specs"

## Notable Gap

The webpage itself does **not** include actual credentials, API keys, endpoint URLs, or detailed schemas. Those technical specifics appear to be housed in the external GitHub repositories and documentation links provided.

content.thinking:
Now I have concrete data to work with â€” twenty services with actual schemas and pricing models, authentication handled through camel-jwt with support for multiple auth methods, payment processing via x402, lightning, and ecash, and a proof chain built on Nostr NIP-33 kinds. I've got the real API endpoints and the SDK repository, so I can start building out all three deliverables.

I'm organizing everything in a new `blindoracle-agent/` folder and planning to create a manifest for a sample agent (game-reviewer-agent to match the project's theme), a Node.js client that serves its own manifest and capabilities endpoints while posting job intents to the API, and a self-contained HTML explorer page that browses live agents and lets users submit job intents with attestation display. Let me first check what's already in the project to see if there's existing work in the chainlink-agent-swarm folder.

content.text: Got the real schemas. Now checking if there's existing related work in `chainlink-agent-swarm/`.

content.thinking: The shell is expanding `$_.` incorrectly due to glob patterns â€” I need to switch to using KillShell directly on the background process instead.

content.text:
All three deliverables are in place and verified working end-to-end.

**1. Manifest** â€” `blindoracle-agent/manifest.json` â€” a v1 manifest for a `games.game-reviewer-agent`, conforming to the real schema observed at `api.craigmbrown.com/a2a/manifest` (auth block, payment_rails, proof_chain kinds, services[] with input/output JSON Schemas, SLA, pricing, bond tier).

**2. Agent** â€” `blindoracle-agent/agent.js` â€” a zero-dep Node.js service:
- `GET /a2a/manifest` and `GET /a2a/capabilities` â€” derived from the manifest
- `POST /a2a/jobs` â€” accepts a job-intent envelope, fetches the prompt URL or uses inline source, runs a heuristic HTML-game review, emits a `ProofOfWork` (Nostr kind 30010) and `ProofOfResolution` (kind 30015), enforces budget vs price
- `GET /a2a/proofs` â€” in-memory ProofDB
- `GET /remote/{manifest,capabilities}` â€” proxies the live `api.craigmbrown.com`
- Smoke test passed: posted a malformed-HTML prompt â†’ got 4 findings + 4 fixes + both attestations in 5ms

**3. Explorer** â€” `blindoracle-explorer.html` (root, single self-contained file per project convention) â€” browses agents from either the live API or the local agent, filters/inspects/inputs+outputs schemas, and submits job-intent envelopes from the browser. It expects the live API to fail on `/a2a/jobs` due to auth (camel-jwt / lnurl-auth / nip-98) and tells you to switch to the local agent for the unauthenticated demo.

To run the demo:
```
node blindoracle-agent/agent.js                  # in one terminal
npx browser-sync start --server --files "*.html" --port 3000
# open http://localhost:3000/blindoracle-explorer.html
```

content: Goal was to integrate with BlindOracle for Agents â€” built a manifest, a Node.js agent server with job-intent and proof attestations, and a self-contained HTML explorer, all verified end-to-end. Next: open blindoracle-explorer.html in the browser to try it. (disable recaps in /config)

message.content: give me ideas on things i should add to the game that i haven't already

content.thinking:
Looking at what's already in the monster-hunter game, I can see there's a substantial foundation with the expansive world, multiple biomes, and a sophisticated monster AI system with various behavioral states. I should focus on suggesting features that would build naturally on this existing architecture.

The most impactful additions would be weapon variety with different attack patterns and ranges, a carving system to reward monster defeats with loot, and trap mechanics that add tactical depth to hunts. Beyond that, stamina management, dodge rolls with invulnerability frames, and a skill tree or mantles system would deepen the combat feel. Cooking could tie into the existing Items tab, and endemic life scattered throughout biomes would add visual richness and environmental storytelling.

For structure, a quest system with NPCs and faction reputation would give hunts more purpose, while caves and verticality would expand exploration. Monster variants (tempered, elder) and turf wars between creatures would keep encounters fresh. Quality-of-life features like multiple save slots, a settings menu, gamepad support, and audio would round things outâ€”though audio in a single HTML file is tricky, so that's worth flagging as a lower priority.

content.text:
Assuming you mean `monster-hunter.html` (the deepest one). Skipping anything already in your memory dump â€” here's what would have the highest return for the world you've already built.

**Combat depth**
- Stamina bar â€” gates dodge-roll (i-frames), heavy attack, sprint. Forces rhythm instead of mash-J.
- 2â€“3 weapon types with different range/speed/element (sword, bow, hammer). Switch at camp.
- Charge attack on hold-J; combo finisher if you land 3 hits in a window.
- Monster part breaking â€” horn/wing/tail flags on each monster; broken parts change AI (no ranged after wing break) and drop unique loot.

**Hunt loop / progression**
- Carve corpses for crafting mats; gear tiers unlock at camp (Iron â†’ Bone â†’ Volcanic â†’ Void).
- Quest board in camp: 3 rotating contracts ("hunt 1 desert beast", "gather 5 honey"), reward = mats + zenny.
- Trap crafting: pitfall + shock trap, placeable in world, monster AI must path around them.
- Mantle/skill slots â€” passive perks unlocked by killing N of a species (e.g. "Burn res +50% after 5 Volcanic kills").

**World life**
- Endemic critters (frogs, beetles, herbs) you can net for buffs â€” gives non-combat reasons to wander.
- Turf wars: when two monsters' patrol radii overlap, they fight each other. You get a free chunk of damage if you watch.
- Apex/Tempered variants â€” rare-spawn versions of existing 15 monsters with stat mods + unique drop. Tracked in Bestiary.
- Monster nests with eggs you can steal (alerts mother â†’ forced fight).

**Traversal**
- Climbable cliff faces between biomes â€” gives vertical layer to your 8000px strip.
- Hidden caves behind waterfalls/boulders, each with a chest + 1 elder spawn.
- Fast-travel between discovered camps once you build a second camp at, say, x=4000.

**Survival flavor (cheap to add, big feel)**
- Hunger/cold meter that night/biome accelerates â€” eat carved meat at a campfire.
- Cooking minigame: timing-based, well-done = bigger HP + stat buff.

**Polish that punches above its weight**
- Photo mode: pause, free-cam, hide HUD, screenshot via canvas.toBlob.
- Audio: even just 4 WebAudio loops (calm/forest/combat/night) layered by state changes the game completely.
- Speedrun timer + run recap on death (monsters killed, parts broken, distance traveled).
- Gamepad support â€” one `navigator.getGamepads()` poll covers it.

Want me to pick one and implement it? Stamina + dodge-roll is the smallest change with the biggest combat-feel delta; part-breaking is the biggest "real game" upgrade.

lastPrompt: give me ideas on things i should add to the game that i haven't already
